channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Senegal's Constitutional Council Blocks Bill to Limit Presidential Powers
item.description: The Senegalese Constitutional Council has ruled against a bill proposed by the PASTEF party, which aimed to reduce President Bassirou Diomaye Faye's influence and transfer more power to Ousmane Sonko....

item.title: Kizimkazi Festival 2026 Promotes Tourism and Cultural Heritage
item.description: The Kizimkazi Festival 2026 is set to become a major event showcasing the region's rich cultural heritage, tourism attractions, and investment potential. Scheduled from August 12-14 in South Unguja, t...

item.title: OpenAI Designates GPT 5.6 as Preferred Model for Microsoft Copilot 365
item.description: OpenAI has announced that its GPT 5.6 model will serve as the 'preferred model' for Microsoft's Copilot 365, addressing recent speculation about a potential partnership rift. This decision comes after...

item.title: Overseas Jobs Empower Kericho Youth to Overcome Unemployment Challenges
item.description: Young people in Bomet County, Kenya, are finding new hope through overseas employment initiatives that are breaking the cycle of unemployment. Despite graduating with degrees and diplomas, many faced ...

item.title: MeTL expands manufacturing with Sh7.8 trillion revenue target and graphite processing focus
item.description: MeTL Group, a Tanzanian conglomerate, aims to exceed Sh7.8 trillion in annual revenue as it transitions from trading to manufacturing. CEO Mohammed Dewji highlighted the company's growth through decad...

item.title: Recruitment irregularities in Homa Bay assistant chiefs under scrutiny
item.description: The recruitment of five assistant chiefs in Homa Bay Sub-county has sparked concerns over transparency and delays in receiving appointment letters. Despite following Public Service Commission (PSC) re...

item.title: Telstra outage, political tensions and UNRWA scrutiny highlight modern connectivity challenges
item.description: The article explores the multifaceted impacts of recent events on Australian society. A major Telstra outage disrupted thousands, exposing vulnerabilities in critical infrastructure. Simultaneously, a...

item.title: Pakistan's Operation Shaban Targets Terrorists in Balochistan, 39 Militants Killed in 24 Hours
item.description: Security forces in Balochistan have killed at least 39 India-backed terrorists during Operation Shaban over the past 24 hours, according to official sources. The military operation, launched after an ...

item.title: Nobel Laureate Chemist Joins Tsinghua University's AI Institute in China
item.description: Dr. Omar Yaghi, a Nobel-winning chemist renowned for his groundbreaking work on metal-organic frameworks (MOFs), has accepted an offer to lead a new artificial intelligence institute at Tsinghua Unive...

item.title: Amarnath Yatra faces early ice lingam melting due to climate change concerns
item.description: The Amarnath Yatra, which began on July 1, 2026, has witnessed the rapid melting of the sacred Baba Barfani ice lingam, with over 90% of its mass disappearing within days. This unprecedented rate of m...

item.title: England Players' Reactions to Ed Sheeran's Private Gig at World Cup Camp
item.description: The article details the lukewarm reactions of England's World Cup squad to a private gig performed by singer Ed Sheeran at their training base in Kansas City. Footage from the event, shared via Englan...

item.title: Kenya Weather Forecast: Rain and Cold Spells Expected Across Nairobi and Other Counties This Weekend
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department has issued a forecast predicting rainfall, cloud cover, and cooler temperatures across several counties, including Nairobi, over the weekend of July 10-12. Nairobi ...

item.title: Lateef Adedimeji Explains Why He Cries In Movies To Stand Out From Odunlade Adekola
item.description: Nollywood actor Lateef Adedimeji has shared how his signature crying scenes in movies helped him stand out from his colleague Odunlade Adekola. During an interview with Morayo Afolabi Brown on her You...

item.title: Bonnie Tyler (1951–2026): Welsh singer and rock icon who defined a generation
item.description: Bonnie Tyler, the iconic Welsh singer known for her raspy voice and powerful performances, has passed away at the age of 75 following a short illness. Her career spanned decades, marked by hits like '...

item.title: Rare dolphin sightings in B.C. linked to El Niño and climate change
item.description: A rare sighting of long-beaked common dolphins has been reported in British Columbia's waters, marking an extraordinary event due to their rarity in temperate regions. Jackie Hildering of the Marine E...

item.title: France advances to World Cup semifinals after dominant win over Morocco
item.description: France secured a 2-0 victory against Morocco in their World Cup quarterfinal clash, advancing to the semifinals with confidence. Coach Didier Deschamps emphasized the team's resilience despite missed ...

item.title: Maharashtra forms seven-member panel to draft Uniform Civil Code
item.description: Maharashtra has formed a seven-member committee headed by retired Supreme Court judge Ranjana Prakash Desai to prepare a draft for implementing a Uniform Civil Code in the state. Chief Minister Devend...

item.title: Barbie Almalbis Clarifies Barbie’s Cradle Never Broke Up, Emphasizes Music as Bond
item.description: Barbie Almalbis, a prominent Filipino musician, recently addressed rumors about the dissolution of her band Barbie’s Cradle. In an interview, she clarified that the group never officially broke up, em...

item.title: South Africa's Orange Basin Energy Deal Faces Geopolitical Tensions
item.description: The article discusses the tension between South Africa's commercial energy decisions and its geopolitical stance, particularly regarding an Israeli firm's involvement in the Orange Basin oil explorati...

item.title: Fidji Simo Steps Down from OpenAI’s Leadership Role Amid Health Challenges
item.description: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's second-in-command, is transitioning to a part-time advisory role after her prolonged medical leave due to a neuroimmune condition. Her departure marks a significant shift for the ...
